title: Ensure that the Kubernetes PKI certificate file permissions are set to 644 or more restrictive

input_row.title: Ensure that the Kubernetes PKI certificate file permissions are set to 644 or more restrictive
input_row.description: Ensure that Kubernetes PKI certificate files have permissions of 644 or more restrictive.
input_row.rationale: Kubernetes makes use of a number of certificate files as part of the operation of its components. The permissions on these files should be set to 644 or more restrictive to protect their integrity and confidentiality.
input_row.audit: Run the below command (based on the file location on your system) on the Control Plane node. For example, stat -c '%a' /etc/kubernetes/pki/*.crt Verify that the permissions are 644 or more restrictive. or ls -l /etc/kubernetes/pki/*.crt Verify -rw------
input_row.remediation: Run the below command (based on the file location on your system) on the Control Plane node. For example, chmod -R 644 /etc/kubernetes/pki/*.crt
input_row.default_value: By default, the certificates used by Kubernetes are set to have permissions of 644

step1_initial_assessment:
**AUDIT APPROACH:** Manual

**MANUAL STEPS:**
1. SSH into the Control Plane node where the Kubernetes PKI certificate files are stored.
2. Run the command: `ls -l /etc/kubernetes/pki/*.crt`
3. Verify the permissions of the listed certificate files.
4. Ensure that the permissions are set to `644` or more restrictive (e.g., `600`).
5. If any file has permissions that are not `644` or more restrictive, it is a compliance issue. 

Expected secure configuration: All certificate files should have permissions of `644` or more restrictive. 

To confirm compliance, ensure that all files listed in the output of the command meet the expected permission criteria.
